definition_owner: The first year of the unbroken light era: the dawn flickered for 11 years (-4088 one light year, then dark returned; last dark year -4079), and from -4078 the light half held EVERY year - 10,501 consecutive years, until +6423 CE. No averaging; the raw measured series.

light_era.note: Around +6423-6432 CE the reverse flicker happens and the DARK era begins (~10k years); the NEXT Anno Lucis is +16429 - one apsidal cycle later.

superseded_smoothed_estimate.note: the 71-yr rolling-mean crossing - kept for the record; the owner ruled the raw never-again definition is THE definition (2026-07-16)

delta_t_caveat: Event YEARS and season DURATIONS are robust; exact local clock times over +/-15 millennia are not (the Earth-rotation deltaT model carries hours of uncertainty).
